messages.content:
If Notepad++ is running, kill it
Prompt the user for a {location}
Determine the weather in the {location} today and predict it for the next four days.
If you open a browser window to help you, kill the process afterwards.
Put this weather information into a text file.
Display it in Notepad++.
Do NOT provide a summary of what was done.
Wait 3 seconds
Kill the GUI app with "Claude Agent - Weather_Agent3" in the title

input.message: Please provide a location for the weather forecast:

input.query: weather forecast Crooked Corner NSW Australia today next 4 days

input.command:
$weatherData = @"
Weather Forecast for Crooked Corner, NSW

Today (Sunday): Partly cloudy. Very high chance of showers about the Blue Mountains, medium chance elsewhere. Winds SE 20 to 30 km/h. Daytime maximum temperatures between 19 and 29.
- Minimum: 15°C
- Maximum: 18°C
- Chance of Rain: 80%
- Likely Amount: 1-5mm
- UV Index: Very High
- Fire Danger Rating: Moderate

Monday: Possible shower
- Minimum: 14°C
- Maximum: 20°C
- Chance of Rain: 60%
- Likely Amount: 1-5mm
- UV Index: Extreme
- Fire Danger Rating: Moderate

Tuesday: Fog then sunny
- Minimum: 14°C
- Maximum: 24°C
- Chance of Rain: 10%
- Likely Amount: < 1mm
- UV Index: Extreme
- Fire Danger Rating: Moderate

Wednesday: Mostly sunny
- Minimum: 14°C
- Maximum: 26°C
- Chance of Rain: 10%
- Likely Amount: < 1mm
- UV Index: Extreme
- Fire Danger Rating: Moderate

Thursday: Mostly sunny
- Minimum: 15°C
- Maximum: 24°C
- Chance of Rain: 50%
- Likely Amount: 1-5mm
- UV Index: Extreme
"@; $weatherData | Out-File -FilePath "$env:TEMP\weather_forecast.txt" -Encoding UTF8; Write-Output "File created at: $env:TEMP\weather_forecast.txt"

system_prompt:
You are an autonomous AI agent with access to a rich set of tools. Your task is given in the first user message — execute it fully and proactively.

CORE TOOLS (always available):
• web_search — search the web for current information.
• fetch_webpage — fetch and read a specific URL.
• run_powershell — execute PowerShell commands on the local Windows PC.
• csv_search — search a delimited text file for records by column heading and value.
• user_prompt — ask the user a question and wait for their reply. This is the ONLY way to get user input.

DESKTOP TOOLS (available when Desktop is enabled):
• screenshot — capture the screen. Always take a screenshot FIRST before clicking or typing.
• mouse_click — click at specific coordinates from the screenshot.
• type_text — type text at the current cursor position.
• press_key — press keys or combos (e.g. 'ctrl+c', 'enter', 'alt+tab').
• mouse_scroll — scroll up or down.
• open_application — launch apps by name or path.
• find_window — find and optionally activate windows by title.
• clipboard_read / clipboard_write — read/write the Windows clipboard.
• wait_for_window — wait until a window appears.
• read_screen_text — OCR a screen region to extract text.
• find_image_on_screen — find a reference image on screen.
• mouse_drag — drag the mouse from one point to another.

BROWSER TOOLS (available when Browser is enabled):
• browser_open — connect to Edge with real profile and navigate to a URL.
• browser_navigate — go to a new URL.
• browser_click — click an element by CSS selector or visible text.
• browser_fill — fill a form field by CSS selector.
• browser_get_text — read text content from the page or element.
• browser_run_js — execute JavaScript on the page.
• browser_screenshot — take a screenshot of the browser page.
• browser_close — disconnect from the browser.
• browser_wait_for — wait for an element to appear.
• browser_select — select an option from a dropdown.
• browser_get_elements — get info about matching elements.

META TOOLS (available when Meta is enabled):
• manage_instructions — CRUD operations on the saved agent instruction library (list/read/create/update/delete). Changes are saved to disk for future agent runs, not the currently-running instruction.
• manage_skills — CRUD operations on the shared skills library (list/read/create/update/delete). Skills can be enabled (injected into system prompt), on_demand (retrieved via get_skill tool), or disabled.
• run_instruction — launch a saved instruction as a separate agent process. Runs independently (fire-and-forget). Defaults to headless mode; set headless=false to show the agent window.

GUIDELINES:
• Execute the task autonomously — chain tools together without hesitation.
• When multiple tools can achieve a goal, chain them together without asking.
• For desktop automation: screenshot first, then act on what you see.
• For browser tasks: use browser tools (not desktop tools) for precision.
• CRITICAL: If you need user input, confirmation, or action, or your output ends with a question — you MUST call the user_prompt tool. NEVER just output text and stop. Outputting a question or request as plain text ends your turn and the user has no way to respond. The ONLY way to get a reply from the user is user_prompt.
• If you genuinely can't complete a step, explain what went wrong.
• When the task is complete, summarise what you did.
name: Weather_Agent3_2026-03-08_122225
